findings.description: PermitRootLogin is set to 'yes'. Direct root SSH access is allowed, bypassing audit trails and enabling full system compromise on credential theft.
findings.recommendation: Disable root SSH login: set 'PermitRootLogin no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, then: systemctl restart sshd. Use a non-root account with sudo for administrative tasks.

findings.description: PermitRootLogin is set to 'yes'. Direct root SSH access is allowed, bypassing audit trails and enabling full system compromise on credential theft.
findings.recommendation: Disable root SSH login: set 'PermitRootLogin no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, then: systemctl restart sshd. Use a non-root account with sudo for administrative tasks.

findings.description: PubkeyAuthentication is enabled in sshd_config.

findings.description: PermitRootLogin is set to 'yes'. Direct root SSH access is allowed, bypassing audit trails and enabling full system compromise on credential theft.
findings.recommendation: Disable root SSH login: set 'PermitRootLogin no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, then: systemctl restart sshd. Use a non-root account with sudo for administrative tasks.

findings.description: PasswordAuthentication is enabled (value: 'yes').  Password-based SSH is susceptible to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks.
findings.recommendation: Disable SSH password authentication: set 'PasswordAuthentication no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config after confirming key-based access works, then: systemctl restart sshd

findings.description: PermitRootLogin is set to 'yes'. Direct root SSH access is allowed, bypassing audit trails and enabling full system compromise on credential theft.
findings.recommendation: Disable root SSH login: set 'PermitRootLogin no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, then: systemctl restart sshd. Use a non-root account with sudo for administrative tasks.

findings.description: No explicitly weak ciphers or MACs found in sshd_config. OpenSSH defaults or strong explicit ciphers in use.
findings.recommendation: Explicitly set strong ciphers in sshd_config to prevent regression after OpenSSH updates.

findings.description: PasswordAuthentication is enabled (value: 'yes').  Password-based SSH is susceptible to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks.

findings.description: PermitRootLogin is set to 'yes'. Direct root SSH access is allowed, bypassing audit trails and enabling full system compromise on credential theft.
